summary: Scorching Scarlet > Fire, soaring Black Peter and tight drums
blurb: The second set erupts with a monster Scarlet > Fire transition that feeds into an explosive Iko Iko jam. Jerry’s wah‑filled solos and the dual‑drum onslaught after Estimated Prophet keep the energy relentless. Highlights include Black Peter, He's Gone and a tight, vocal‑rich first set.
review: May 7, 1978 at RPI’s Field House captures one of the spring ’78 high points. The band launches the night with a punchy first set – Jack Straw, Dire Wolf and a bright Friend Of The Devil – before diving into an incendiary second half. Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain blazes with Garcia’s soaring wah‑solo and Donna Jean’s soaring harmonies, segueing into an immaculate Estimated Prophet → He's Gone that builds into a thunderous drum break and the legendary Iko Iko jam. The Other One stretches out while the rhythm section—Mickey and Bill—deliver what reviewers call “the wildest drums ever heard.” Black Peter closes the set with a three‑minute, emotionally charged solo that leaves the audience chanting. Across all sources the show is praised for its relentless momentum, tight transitions and the band’s collective fire, making it a must‑listen snapshot of ’78 Dead energy.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings are strongly positive, two mixed; multiple reviewers repeatedly praise the Scarlet>Fire transition, Iko>Iko drum jam, Black Peter solo and overall band tightness, yielding a solid positive sentiment across sources.

best_recording.reason: Crisp soundboard mix with clear instrument separation; captures the high‑energy performance despite minor drop‑outs, making it the most complete and sonically satisfying source.

key_highlights: Monster Scarlet > Fire transition that fuels the second set
Explosive Iko Iko drum jam highlighted as "the wildest drums"
Soaring Black Peter solo with emotional vocal delivery
Tight dual‑drum segment after Estimated Prophet

band_performance.Jerry: Blazing wah‑filled solos, especially on Scarlet>Fire and Black Peter; powerful vocals and stage presence (white lights at He's Gone, hula participation in Iko Iko).
band_performance.Phil: Delivers deep, bomb‑like bass lines that anchor the jams, notably during The Other One.
band_performance.Bob: Slide guitar shines, competing with Garcia on Fire and adding a distinctive flavor throughout the set.
band_performance.Mickey: Part of the “wildest drums” praised; drives the high‑energy drum break after Estimated Prophet.
band_performance.Billy: Pairs with Mickey for an outstanding dual‑drum segment that propels the second half.
